name: Salt Trail
name_original: La Ruta del Salt  — Desde Náquera a la Font de l'Or (PR-CV 329)
description: Hike the moderate 5.7-mile Route of the Jump (PR-CV 329) in Serra Calderona Natural Park, exploring forest regeneration and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Náquera offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, characterized by ravines and abundant forests of pines and cork oaks. The mountainous terrain provides panoramic views of the Gulf of Valencia. Trails in the region often feature historical elements such as old trenches and water constructions, adding cultural interest to the natural beauty. Best hiking trails around Náquera The most popular hiking route is Salt Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route explores forest regeneration and leads to the monumental Pi del Salt. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castell de Serra – View of Serra loop from Nàquera / Náquera, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This trail offers expansive views from the historic Castell de Serra over the Turia Valley and towards the coast. Local hikers also love the Montcúdio Viewpoint – El Pi del Salt loop from Nàquera / Náquera, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Náquera is defined by mountainous terrain, ravines, and dense pine and cork oak forests, offering panoramic views. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Náquera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Náquera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Náquera?
A: Náquera offers a wide variety of hiking experiences with over 40 trails. These routes cater to different fitness levels, including 15 easy, 26 moderate, and 3 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Náquera?
A: Hiking around Náquera primarily involves mountainous terrain within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. You'll encounter diverse landscapes featuring ravines, dense forests of pines and cork oaks, and sometimes rocky areas. Many trails offer breathtaking panoramic views of the Gulf of Valencia and the surrounding valleys.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Náquera?
A: Yes, Náquera has several easy trails suitable for families. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5308397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcúdio Viewpoint – El Pi del Salt loop from Nàquera / Náquera&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.0 miles (6.5 km) long and takes about 1 hour 52 minutes. Another accessible choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14710657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de l'Oro – El Pi del Salt loop from Nàquera / Náquera&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.3 km route with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Náquera?
A: While the Sierra Calderona Natural Park is generally welcoming to dogs, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas, to respect wildlife and other hikers. Please ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Náquera?
A: Many trails around Náquera are designed as loops, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Serra – View of Serra loop from Nàquera / Náquera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.5-mile (12.1 km) circular path that provides expansive views from the historic castle.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting landmarks and enjoy stunning viewpoints. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Serra&lt;/a&gt; offers grandiose views of the Sierra Calderona and the Turia Valley. You can also find natural features like the monumental Pi del Salt. Other points of interest include old trenches and historical water constructions like the Aljub de la Patà.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Náquera?
A: The hiking trails in Náquera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 220 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the panoramic views of the Gulf of Valencia, and the well-maintained paths that lead through pine and cork oak forests.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking areas in Náquera?
A: Náquera is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services from Valencia. While buses can get you to the town, access to specific trailheads within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park might require a short walk or local taxi service. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Náquera?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Náquera town center. For specific trailheads within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, there are often designated parking areas. However, during peak seasons or weekends, these can fill up quickly, so arriving early is recommended.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, many trails near Náquera feature historical elements. You can encounter old trenches, the Aljub de la Patà (a historical water construction), and the Sifó Mine. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Serra&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th-century fortress, is a prominent historical landmark offering insights into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Náquera?
A: The best time to hike in Náquera is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are generally mild, some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any gorges or canyons to explore near Náquera?
A: Yes, the region around Náquera features several impressive gorges and ravines. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4241420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Carraixet&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2785413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PortaCeli Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, which offer unique geological formations and scenic views. These natural features add another layer of adventure to the hiking experience.
tips: Nice panoramic point next to the main path with very nice views.

One of the points of interest on the Ruta del Salt where you can recharge your bottle of water or sit down to enjoy a coffee or a drink at the bar next door. The area has parking, some tables and a beautiful grove, a good alternative to start your route from here.

The pine tree, approximately 150 years old, is located among the ruins of an old quarry. According to comments from some locals, the pine was about to be cut on more than one occasion (it is still possible to see the healed wounds). Thanks to the stonecutters of the time, who came to its defense against the various attacks, the pine still remains here.
It is in a picnic area with tables and ample space. The visit is essential if you are in the area

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this route and what is its difficulty level?
faqs.answer: The Route of the Jump is generally considered to be of low to moderate difficulty, making it suitable for beginners. It typically takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes to complete the full circuit.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail officially starts from the town hall square in Náquera, Valencia. Parking is generally available in Náquera village, making it easily accessible for hikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse landscapes within the Serra Calderona Natural Park. The path includes sections along firebreaks and forest tracks, with some ascents and descents. You'll see areas of forest regeneration after a 2004 fire, abandoned red sandstone quarries, and geological formations. It's also known as a 'botanical trail' with interpretive signs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Montcúdio Viewpoint, offering panoramic views, the natural spring Font de l'Oro where you can refill water, and the notable pine tree El Pi del Salt. You'll also pass the Barranc de l'Arquet and the Canteras de El Salt.

faqs.question: Is The Route of the Jump — From Náquera to the Font de l'Or (PR-CV 329) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly, as it's located within the Serra Calderona Natural Park. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash to protect wildlife and respect other hikers, and remember to carry out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public within the Serra Calderona Natural Park.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other paths. Notably, it follows parts of the Camí del Salt, and smaller portions of the Camino de Segart and the Senda Barranc del Salt.

faqs.question: Is there a natural water source available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Font de l'Oro is a natural spring located along the route, where many hikers stop to fill their water bottles. It's always wise to carry sufficient water, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: What should I know about the trail's maintenance and signage?
faqs.answer: While the trail is well-established and promoted by the Náquera Town Council, some sources suggest that recent quality control checks on signage and maintenance might be lacking. It's advisable to carry a map or use a GPS device like komoot to navigate, especially if you're unfamiliar with the area.

faqs.question: What regulations should I be aware of when hiking in Serra Calderona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As the trail is within the Serra Calderona Natural Park, general park regulations apply. These typically include staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife or plants, carrying out all trash, and being mindful of fire restrictions, especially during dry seasons. Dogs must be kept on a leash.
